'''Harvey Livett''' (born 4 January 1997) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward or {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]], and the [[England Knights]] at international level.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He also played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Super League.

==Background==
He is the son of the rugby league {{rlp|ce}} who played in the 1990s and 2000s for [[Workington Town]], and [[Woolston Rovers]] ARLFC; Peter Livett.

==Career==
===Warrington Wolves===
In 2017, he made his [[Super League]] début for the [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] club against the [[Catalans Dragons]], having previously played in pre-season friendlies.

Livett made a short appearance during the 2017 World Club Series match against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in which Warrington were victors 27–18. He then became an important part of the Warrington team during 2017, scoring two tries and converting 10 goals.  In one match, he scored a try and kicked six goals in a 40–18 victory against St Helens. Livett played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat to the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}}</ref>
On 17 June 2019, Livett joined fellow [[Super League]] side [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a months loan.

===Salford===
On 11 December 2020 it was announced that Livett would join Salford on a two-year deal for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55281805|title=Harvey Livett: Salford Red Devils sign Warrington back-row forward|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=13 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 12 October 2022, it was announced that Livett would join [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a three-year deal for the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/harvey-livett-outlines-trophy-ambitions-after-joining-huddersfield|title=Harvey Livett outlines trophy ambitions after joining Huddersfield|website=Love Rugby League|accessdate=12 Oct 2022|date=12 Oct 2022}}</ref>
Livett only featured in eight matches for Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>
Livett was limited to only eight appearances with Huddersfield in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish 9th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-34-castleford-tigers-10-hosts-win-dead-rubber-but-2024-season-one-to-forget-for-west-yorkshire-rivals-4789784/|title=Huddersfield Giants 34 Castleford Tigers 10: Hosts win dead rubber but 2024 season one to forget for West Yorkshire rivals|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>.It was announced in December 2024 that Livett had left the Giants after playing 17 times.<ref>https://www.giantsrl.com/article/9091/salabio-and-livett-to-depart</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref> Later that year he was selected for the England Knights on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the [[PNG Football Stadium|Oil Search National Football Stadium]].<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>
